body_preview:
Linda,

Thank you for the advance notice regarding Christine's visit. The timing is, in fact, quite workable. The Junior Open planning committee meets Friday evening, not over the weekend, so both Saturday and Sunday remain unencumbered on my end.

Regard
subject: Re: Linda

body_preview:
Eric,

I write to bring to your attention an opportunity that may be of interest to young Tony. The Federation has confirmed its Summer Junior Invitational, scheduled for the second Saturday of July at the Silver Spring Civic Center. The section for playe
subject: Eric

body_preview:
Dad,

Thank you for thinking of Tony  -  he will be thrilled. Diane and I talked it over last night and we are both in favor. You flagged his interest at exactly the right moment; he has been watching YouTube videos of grandmaster games and asking questions
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:
Eric,

Excellent. I have submitted his registration this morning and confirmed with the tournament director. His pairing card will list him as Anthony T. Nguyen III unless you prefer a different rendering  -  advise me if so.

The walk is set for the Friday
subject: Re: Eric

subject: Dear Christine

He has been
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:

Christine mentioned that you have been deep in preparation for the Federation's spring scholastic tournament, and I wanted to reach out before the registration window closes. I hope you and Linda are both keeping well.

Tony Jr. has been pract
subject: Dear Tony

body_preview:
Dear Robert,

I appreciate you writing ahead of the registration deadline  -  it reflects the kind of foresight that makes logistics manageable. The MACCF Spring Scholastic Exhibition is scheduled for Saturday, 28 June, at the Silver Spring Civic Center. We
subject: Re: Dear Robert

body_preview:
Tony,

Thank you for the thorough breakdown  -  that is exactly what I needed. We will get Tony Jr. registered in the Novice section before the weekend. The G/30 time control sounds entirely manageable; I will be honest, I had some concern about how he woul
subject: Re: Tony

body_preview:
Dear Tony,

I am writing to bring to your attention an opportunity I believe would serve you well. The Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation is hosting the Sligo Creek Open on the weekend of July 12th, and I have identified a spot in the Junior Division
subject: Dear Tony

body_preview:
Hi Grandpa,

Thank you so much for thinking of me  -  I actually do want to try a real tournament! I have been working on the Ruy Lopez opening you showed me last time and I think it is starting to click. Friday to register is no problem at all.

Quick ques
subject: Re: Hi Grandpa

body_preview:
Dear Tony,

Your enthusiasm is noted and, I will admit, gratifying. The Ruy Lopez is an excellent choice of study  -  it has served grandmasters and club players alike for over four centuries, a fact that speaks plainly to its soundness.

To your question:
subject: Re: Dear Tony

body_preview:
Hi Grandpa,

Six rounds  -  okay, that is a lot more than I expected, but I am excited. I did not realize the Ruy Lopez was that old. That actually makes me want to study it even more seriously.

Thursday after lunch works perfectly for me. And I was wonder
subject: Re: Hi Grandpa
